issuetype.description: A big user story that needs to be broken down. Created by Jira Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Feature

content.text: Currently when create report based a report template, it will go to data retrieval mode by default. In this feature, we will have an option for user to select to go to data pause mode or data retrieval mode.
| Answer Prompt first, and go to Data Pause Mode | Answer Prompt first, and go to Data Retrieval Mode
| Answer Prompt first, and go to Data Pause Mode | Answer Prompt first, and go to Data Retrieval Mode
| Directly open Data Pause Mode | Directly go to Data Retrieval Mode
QA Plan: BCIN-7044 Create Library Report from Template Enhancement
Currently, when a user creates a report based on a report template, Library Web automatically enters
to trigger prompt resolution. Users cannot choose to stay in
, and this behavior differs from BIWeb and Developer.
This feature introduces a mode selector when creating a report from a template, allowing users to choose between
Create Library Report from Template Enhancement
All deployment types (MCE, MEP, MCG, VMWare Tanzu, Container)
When creating a report from a template in Library Web (25.11):
whether they start in Pause (design) mode or Execution mode.
Users cannot select the edit mode (Pause / Execution) before entering the report editor.
Prompts in templates cannot be resolved while staying in
When creating a report from a template, the user can choose whether the report opens in
. Behavior varies by report template type:
Answer prompt first, then open in
Answer prompt first, then open in
Answer prompt first, then open in
Answer prompt first, then open in
* Verify complete create-from-template workflow in both Pause and Execution modes
* Validate prompt resolution flow in Pause mode (new behavior)
* Validate mode selection persistence through prompt resolution
* Verify cancel prompt behavior in create-from-template flow
* Validate new createFromTemplate and editWithData props work correctly
* Verify I-Server execution flag changes (180228 vs 49156)
* Validate prompt editor integration in Pause mode
* Verify checkbox "Edit with execution mode" UI in dossier creator
* Validate smooth prompt resolution without unexpected loading states
* Verify cancel prompt navigates back appropriately (Library home / close window)
* Validate no UI flicker during mode initialization
* Measure impact of extra rebuildDocument manipulation
* Compare E2E performance: Pause mode vs Execution mode creation
* Validate no performance degradation for empty templates
* Monitor prompt resolution time in Pause mode
* Verify no security regressions in prompt handling
* Validate session timeout handling during create-from-template
* Verify proper authentication in rebuild manipulations
* Verify "Edit with execution mode" checkbox meets accessibility standards
* Validate keyboard navigation through prompt resolution
* Verify screen reader announcements for mode selection
* MCE (MicroStrategy Cloud Environment) - AWS, Azure, GCP (VM & Container)
* MCG (MicroStrategy Cloud for Government) - FedRAMP (AWS), Container
* Verify backward compatibility: existing create-from-template flows
* Validate I-Server version check for new checkbox visibility
* Test with legacy I-Server versions (should fall back gracefully)
* Verify migration from 25.11 behavior to 26.03 behavior
* Verify "Edit with execution mode" checkbox string translation
* Validate prompt editor strings in all supported languages
* Test RTL language support for new UI elements
* Identify test cases suitable for automation (E2E, regression)
* Verify unit test coverage for new code paths
* Validate mock support for onCancelFromTemplate callback
* document-view.tsx: Core logic for mode selection and prompt resolution
* report-container.tsx: Callback handling and thunk argument updates
* doc-view-slice.ts: State management for resolvePromptOnly flag
* types.ts: Type definitions for new properties
* Added createFromTemplate prop to identify template creation workflow
* Introduced resolvePromptOnly flag for pause mode prompt resolution
* Added onCancelFromTemplate callback for navigation on prompt cancel
[x] Regression: Non-template entry points unaffected (no silent behavior changes due to
* Non-template entry points and prior editWithData consumers (highest priority regression risk)
* All template types (standard, subset, wizard, empty)
* All prompt types (element, value, object, cascading, optional, required)
No open defects. All 4 defects have been resolved and closed.
Time to enter pause mode with prompts
Time to enter execution mode with prompts
Time to enter pause mode without prompts
Time to enter execution mode without prompts
This enhancement does not introduce new security vulnerabilities. Existing authentication, authorization, and data validation mechanisms remain unchanged.
No changes affecting FedRAMP, SOC2, or other compliance certifications.
* (/) Backward compatible: 26.03 Workstation vs 25.12 Library Server: fall back to 25.12 behavior
* (/) No breaking changes to existing APIs
* (!) User behavior change: Default mode switches from execution to pause (more conservative)
* Checkbox label string: "Edit with data" (or final approved string)
* Translation required for all supported languages
* Verify string length doesn't cause UI truncation
It's authoring workflow. No need to support Accessibility.
Test new prompt resolution in pause mode; validate  request uses exef=180228 during resolvePromptOnly
Test backward compatibility and version detection
Report templates with single element prompt
Report templates with multiple cascading prompts
Report templates with value prompts (numeric, text, date)
Report templates with optional and required prompts

fields.summary: Add view mode option for user to select when create report based on report template.
